seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Green Fields and Green Hills Range. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogTitle: Green Fields vs Green Hills Range: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Green Fields and Green Hills Range.
heading: Green Fields vs Green Hills Range — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Green Fields, South Australia and Green Hills Range, South Australia. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Green Fields has a lower overall climate risk score (34/100) compared to Green Hills Range (37/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Green Fields or Green Hills Range safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Green Fields has a flood risk score of 25/100, while Green Hills Range scores 24/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Green Fields scores 28/100 for bushfire risk. Green Hills Range scores 48/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Green Fields or Green Hills Range?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Green Fields has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.07% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
